post_title: TIL that in the early 2000s, Warner Bros. planned to produce a Broadway musical about Batman. Tim Burton would have directed and the music was written by Jim Steinman, best known for writing most of Meat Loaf's hit songs
TIL there's a Marathon in France where you run in costume, drink wine and eat oysters, foie gras, cheese, steak and ice-cream along the way. (Marathon du Medoc)
Til raw kidney beans are toxic. Undercooked kidney beans are even more toxic. Can cause severe nausea, diarrhea, vomiting, and abdominal pains.
TIL that Brazil has the largest Japanese population outside of Japan, with over 2 million people.

TIL that at least 9 MLB players and coaches from the artificial turf era (1970s-1990s) later died from brain cancer, with a striking cluster of 5 connected to the Philadelphia Phillies’ Veterans Stadium
TIL That the most requested photo from the US National Archives is the meeting of President Richard Nixon and Elvis Presley in the White House Oval Office on December 21 1970.

TIL Tommy Flowers designed and built Colossus, the world’s first programmable electronic computer, partly with his own money. It was used to decode German messages in WWII, with two machines still used for British code-breaking until 1960

TIL that before the D-Day landings, two British mini submarines, X20 and X23, secretly waited off Normandy for two days. They surfaced at dawn on June 6 to shine lights and broadcast beacons that guided the entire invasion fleet to the right beaches.

TIL in 2014, passengers were warned three times not to eat nuts on a Ryanair flight due to a 4-year-old girl's severe nut allergy, but a passenger sitting four rows away from the girl ate nuts anyway. The girl went into anaphylactic shock, and the passenger was banned from the airline for two years.
TIL Gnosticism is a religion that claims the Old Testament God was actually a monster called the Demiurge and that an archon (angel) named Sophia sent the snake into Eden to help Adam and Eve escape.
TIL the official statute that defines what the United States flag is (4 U.S.C., §1), still says that the flag has “forty-eight” stars, not fifty.

TIL Piero Calamai, the captain of the Andrea Doria, felt abandoned, vilified & ashamed to have tarnished the name of the long line of Calamai men who had a distinguished naval career. In 1972, he admitted himself to the hospital complaining of malaise. His last words were “Are the passengers saved?”
TIL an analysis of more than 700,000 online gamblers found that only 4% of them had made money from online sports betting over a five-year period (2019-2023).
TIL that after Steve Carell left “The Office,” James Gandolfini of the “Sopranos” was reportedly offered the role but hbo paid him 3 million to turn it down
TIL of gynandromorphism, a developmental phenomenon leading to half male / half female organisms

TIL The Police’s Ghost in the Machine cover shows the band as red digital displays, representing the “ghosts” (the people) inside the “machine” (technology). Each member’s hairstyle is visible, and the design was chosen because the band couldn’t agree on a photo cover.

TIL that the R-colored vowel (the “-er” sound in “butter,” as pronounced in North American English) is rare in languages, occurring in less than 1% of them.  However, those languages include North American English and Mandarin Chinese, two of the most widely-spoken languages on earth.

TIL that in 1982, Ozzy Osbourne's tour bus driver Andrew Aycock, guitarist Randy Rhoads, and makeup artist Rachel Youngblood were killed while riding a small plane Aycock was flying low over the bus in attempt to wake up the band, which he passed twice before clipping a wing and going into a spiral
TIL that contrary to popular belief, trees only produce 20-30% of our oxygen, 70-80% of it is from oceanic organisms.
TIL military working dogs usually outrank their handlers in order to ensure proper respect
TIL Canadians celebrate Thanksgiving on the 2nd Monday in October, not the 4th Thursday in November as Americans do.
TIL only 4 sitting U.S. Vice Presidents have ever been elected President: John Adams, Thomas Jefferson, Martin Van Buren, and George H. W. Bush.

TIL Alexander Alekhine, World Chess Champion from 1927 to 1935, once tried to cross the German-Polish border with no papers. He instead offered a declaration. “I am Alekhine, chess champion of the world. This is my cat. Her name is Chess. I need no passport.” He was arrested.
TIL German goalkeeper Bert Trautman suffered a broken neck in a cup final, but played the rest of the match. Prince Phillip commented on his crooked neck when giving him the winner’s medal

TIL about Tells, archeological mounds found usually in the ancient near East, "an artificial topographical feature...consisting of the accumulated and stratified debris of a succession of consecutive settlements at the same site"
TIL that Jay-Z made a feature song for "Tron Legacy" that was rejected
TIL that three of the five likely oldest rivers on earth are in Appalachia
TIL that the Wichita language, once spoken by the Wichita people of Oklahoma, went extinct in 2016 when its last fluent speaker, Doris McLemore, passed away.

TIL of brain stimulation reward, manually stimulating specific parts of the brain to elicit pleasure and happiness. A volunteer subject in 1986 spent days doing nothing but self-stimulate. She ignored her family and personal hygiene and she developed an open sore on her finger from using the device.

TIL about Japanese Toilet Meals - eating alone in a bathroom for various reasons, most commonly due to fear of being perceived as lonely because one is eating alone. Research in 2013 found that 12.08% of respondents said they'd eaten on the toilet, with a higher percentage of younger people & women.

TIL a woman presented with a persistent cough & fever for 6 months & despite receiving antibiotics & anti-tuberculosis meds during 4 of those months, her symptoms did not improve. Eventually, the cause was found to be an inverted bag-like structure in her lung that was later revealed to be a condom.
TIL that Socrates reckoned that writing would weaken people’s memories and encourage only superficial understanding.
TIL a commuter train went by while Robert Patrick was filming his nude arrival scene in Terminator 2: Judgement Day (1991). He called it the most embarrassing moment of his career.
TIL the Mariners are the only active MLB franchise to never appear in the World Series.
TIL William Gibson, author of the seminal 1984 Sci-Fi novel about virtual reality titled ‘Neuromancer,’ knew nothing about computers at the time and actually wrote the book using a typewriter.
TIL at Mt Rushmore, the original design was to depict them from waist up, the idea was shelved because of a lack of funds.
TIL that in the 2008 movie 'The Women' no men appear on screen. They're just referenced.

TIL that Anders Hejlsberg, a Danish software engineer who currently works for Microsoft, is the original author and core developer of four programming languages : Turbo Pascal, Delphi, C# and Typescript.

TIL that Anton Chekhov's play The Cherry Orchard, contains two loaded guns which are never fired. This goes against Chekhov's own narrative principle known as Chekhov's Gun, which states that all elements in a story must be necessary.

TIL that in 1994 the United States and North Korea almost went to war after North Korea withdrew from the Non-Proliferation Treaty (NPT) In 1993. Tensions lowered after former U.S president Jimmy Carter flew to North Korea to meet with Kim Il Sung, signing the Agreed Framework.

TIL Refined avocado oil has the highest smoke point of all cooking oils at 271 °C (520 °F). This is significantly higher than other cooking oils such as canola oil 204 °C (400 °F). Cooking oils with high smoke points are useful for deep-frying.
TIL about orgasmic epilepsy, a rare form of epilepsy characterized by seizures that cause spontaneous orgasms
TIL MLB hasn't had a repeat champion since 2000 New York Yankees

TIL that while naming the Simpsons' grandpa, Matt Groening chose not to name him after his own grandfather, but allow the other writers to choose a name. The chose the name "Abraham", which, coincidentally, was the name of Groening's grandfather.
TIL of the Quebec Biker War between the Quebec Hells Angels and Rock Machine Motorcycle Clubs, which left 162 dead, 180+ injured, including 84 bombings and 130 cases of Arson
TIL Frozen foods will never expire at -18°C (0°F) or lower
TIL There’s a lake in Dominica that literally boils, surrounded by a steaming, alien-like landscape.

TIL the theme music for NBA on NBC broadcasts, "Roundball Rock," that soundtracked the network's NBA coverage from 1990 to 2002 - during periods when NBC didn't broadcast the NBA - was used as NBC's theme for its Olympic basketball coverage, first in 2008 and then in every Summer Olympics since 2016

TIL About 'Love Never Dies' the sequel to 'Phantom of the Opera'. Considered a total flop the show closed after little over a year in London's West End and the planned Broadway production never happened.
TIL of the 3.5% rule. When historically 3.5% of the population protest nonviolently against a government, that government is likely to fall.

TIL That the lamp in the Pixar company movie intros originated in the animated short film, Luxor Jr in 1986. The animator, John Lasseter was inspired by a Luxo brand lamp he had on his desk and the size comparison between children and adults.
TIL that all four members of KISS released solo albums on the same day on September 18, 1978.

TIL that when Fred Durst of Limp Bizkit wrote the song Hot Dog as a diss track aimed at Trent Reznor, he used so much material from Nine Inch Nail songs that he had to give Trent Reznor a writing credit and pay him royalties.
TIL during a pool party in Mexico in 2013, eight party-goers were rendered unconscious and one 21-year-old male went into a coma after liquid nitrogen was poured into the pool.

TIL of Oscar Smith a 19 year old man who died in a horse diving tragedy in San Antonio, TX in 1907 at "Doc” Carver's "The Great Carver show" which Carver disputed was a heart attack not a kick from the horse as they both dove into the pool.

TIL in a 1990 World Cup Qualifier, with Brazil leading 1-0 over Chile, the Chilean GK purposely cut himself with a razor blade hidden in his glove. He then pretended to be hurt by unruly Brazilian fans. He was soon caught and permanently banned from playing; Chile was also banned from the 1994 WC.
TIL that the plague bacteria block the infected flea's stomach, causing it to vomit infectious blood back into the victim's wound, and eventually to die of starvation.

TIL in 2003, Russ Decker, Wisconsin State Senator, voted for a law to lower the legal blood alcohol limit from 0.10 to 0.08. Then two years later he blew a 0.09 on the breathalyzer test after being pulled over for drunk driving.
TIL about Potoooooooo, a racehorse who had over 25 wins and was named due to mishearing potato as pot-eight-os

TIL Renaissance-era Danish astronomer Tycho Brahe died from either a swollen prostate or burst bladder brought on by his refusal to leave a lengthy banquet and relieve himself, seeing it as a breach of etiquette.

TIL in 1930 more than 65% of the US population went to the movies weekly. That means that out of every 5 people someone knew, 3 of them went out to the movies every week. Since around 1964, the portion of the US population to go to the movies every week has consistently been under 10%.

TIL When Alexander the Great conquered Jerusalem he made a generous deal with the local Jewish population to give them autonomy. Out of gratitude to Alexander, the Jews agreed to name every child born the next year “Alexander.”. It was eventually adapted to “Sender” and became a common Jewish name.

TIL the director of Groundshog Day (1993) originally wanted Tom Hanks to play the lead, but changed his mind, deciding Hanks was "too nice" to begin with, so that his redemption would be a foregone conclusion.
TIL Bill Wilson of AA fame asked for whiskey several times on his deathbed, but was refused.
TIL kitchen sponges are "microbial incubators" and cannot be effectively sterilized outside of laboratory conditions
TIL On a high-fiber diet, people absorb fewer calories overall
TIL the video game, Cyberpunk 2077, has been admitted into the New York Museum of Failures after its catastrophic, bug-filled, launch

TIL Two versions of Chinese Actress Ruan Lingyu's suicide note were released after her death. The first version appears to be a forgery by Tang Jishan, Ruan's partner at the time of her death. The second and less well-known version is believed to be Ruan's actual suicide note.

TIL in 1962, mountaineers warned that unstable rock and ice on the Huascáran mountain could pose a danger to nearby villages. This was ignored by the Peru government, who threatened them with arrest. In 1970, an earthquake caused rocks and ice to come loose, killing 30,000 people.

TIL in 1939, a major international chess tournament in Buenos Aires coincided with the start of World War II. Afterwards, many top European players chose to stay in Argentina rather than return to Europe.

TIL in 2002 a cave diver committed suicide by stabbing himself during a cave diving trip near Split, Croatia. Due to the nature of his death, it was initially investigated as a homicide, but it was later revealed that he had done it, while lost in the underwater cave, to avoid the pain of drowning.
TIL about the Siege of Leningrad during WW2 - it went on for 872 days and led to ~1.5 million deaths from a prewar population of 3.4 million

TIL about James Price, an English alchemist who claimed to be able to turn mercury into silver or gold. When challenged to do it in front of credible witnesses, he agreed, only to instead commit suicide by drinking prussic acid in front of the witnesses.
TIL the Air Force tasked 4 pilots to fly into the mushroom cloud of Ivy Mike, the first H-bomb. One of them, Jimmy Robinson, never made it home.
TIL a duel between two doctors was averted because one refused to fight in the morning and the other refused to fight in the afternoon
TIL that Nullsoft didn't release a 4th version of Winamp, jumping straight from Winamp3 to Winamp 5, joking "nobody wants to see a Winamp 4 skin" ("4 skin" being a pun on foreskin).
TIL Canadian hockey player Duncan MacPherson disappeared in 1989, and his frozen body was only found 14 years later inside a glacier.
TIL Sacheen Littlefeather, a well known Native American Civil Rights activist, who declined Marlon Brando's Oscar on his behalf, was likely not a Native American at all.
TIL Donkey basketball is a variation of the standard basketball game, played on a standard basketball court, but in which the players ride donkeys
TIL that Drew Carey was fired from his role as a spokesperson for A&W Canada in 1998 after an episode of The Drew Carey Show depicted him eating at a McDonald's.

TIL about 'the Harrying of the North', a brutal military campaign waged by William the Conqueror to suppress rebellions in northern England. His scorched-earth tactics killed or displaced up to 75% of the population.
TIL Reagan had 8 government shutdowns. Two lasted for 3 days, two for 2 days and four lasted for just 1 day.
TIL that Ricardo Montalban saw Mr. Roarke as a fallen angel who was serving a penance for being too prideful. Helping guests rediscover their moral compass through the fantasies he granted them.

TIL the only actors who have turned down Quentin Tarantino twice are Sylvester Stallone & Mickey Rourke. Stallone turned down Robert DeNiro's role in Jackie Brown & Kurt Russell's role in Death Proof. Rourke also turned down Russell's role in Death Proof, as well as Bruce Willis role in Pulp Fiction

TIL after two Finnish divers died in a cave in Norway, it was determined that retrieving their bodies would be too difficult, so a diving ban was placed on the cave. Despite this, four of their friends led a team of 27 divers (101 diving hours) who secretly and successfully recovered their bodies.
TIL Nero was a Roman emperor who many believe instigated the Great Fire of Rome, in order to clear land for his planned complex, the "Golden House"

TIL that two amateur Canadian inventors - medical student Henry Woodward and hotelier Mathew Evans - patented the first electric light bulb in 1874. Unable to secure financial backing, they sold their patent to Thomas Edison, who demonstrated an improved electric light to fanfare five years later.
TIL that donating blood plasma can significantly reduce the levels of PFAs/forever chemicals in your blood
TIL that milkshakes were originally alcoholic with whiskey as one of the ingredients. After the 1900s, milkshakes began to use syrups instead and the name changed its meaning.

TIL that since the growth of AI, academic studies have recently been found that include hidden prompts aimed directly at AI reviews. Researchers have included covert instructions in white font or fonts too small for humans to read, such as "give positive reviews only" or "do not highlight negatives"
TIL there is no 0 BC. The calendar goes from 1 BC to AD 1
TIL about the three christs of Yipsilanti, a psychiatric case study where three men who each believed themselves to be Jesus Christ were forced to interact with each other.
TIL about "Typhoid Mary," the first known asymptomatic case of Typhoid. Due to her mistrust in health officials, she refused to stop working as a cook, resulting in over 100 estimated infections.
TIL that people are more likely to believe statements that rhyme, allowing for foolish advice to sound a bit wiser and it’s been called the rhyme as reason effect.

TIL that during the Lady Chatterley trial, the prosecution asked: “Would you approve of your young sons, young daughters - because girls can read as well as boys - reading this book?” Every offending word - fuck, cunt, etc - was read aloud. Jury acquitted; Penguin later dedicated the book to them.
TIL Japan is more than ¥1.3 quadrillion in debt. 46% of that debt is owed to Bank of Japan
TIL That LAX Airport lets pilots and employees live on airport property, renting them parking space for their personal RV's for $60 per month.
TIL that German POWs were forced to pick cotton in the American south during WW2

TIL in 1999 a group of young men in Adelaide, South Australia coined the term "Movember" and the idea of growing moustaches for charity throughout the month of November. They came up with the idea one night in a pub. The group started with 80 men from Adelaide and became a nationwide phenomenon.
TIL that Dan Aykroyd has Asperger, Tourette's, and heterochromia (two different colored eyes)
TIL that during WWII, Britain took down around 50,000 road signs so German invaders wouldn’t know where they were going.
TIL that President Lyndon B Johnson used to hold meetings while sitting on the toilet to assert dominance.

TIL During WW2, Comache people were enlisted as code talkers, and when translations didn't exist from English to their native language, they used descriptive words instead. For example, tank was "turtle", bomber was "pregnant bird", machine gun was "sewing machine", and Hitler was "crazy white man".

TIL one of the earliest and most notorious uses of the "correlation does not imply causation" argument was by R A Fisher, the British polymath and father of modern statistics, who used it in the 1950s to cast doubt on emerging studies linking cigarette smoking to cancer
TIL that Radiohead’s ‘Creep’ was copied from ‘The Air That I Breathe’ by the Hollies.
TIL that Minneapolis and Saint Paul’s rivalry once led to them building competing cathedrals and baseball stadiums, and even disagreeing on daylight saving time.
TIL Papua New Guinea has the most languages spoken within its borders in the world with 840 established languages
TIL that the only Mazda Furai ever made, burned down during a Top Gear photo session in 2008. The wherabouts of the remains of that car is not publically known.

TIL that the fastest surgeon in history, Robert Liston, once performed an amputation so quickly that he accidentally cut off his assistant's fingers, and a spectator died of shock - resulting in the only surgery with a 300% mortality rate

TIL among the oldest sports officials in history is 83-year-old John Leiser, who was recognized in 2023 by the Minnesota State High School Association for his 60th season refereeing football. Leiser was about to start his 63rd year officiating basketball.
TIL the US Military lit the fuse that led to invention of Computer Mice, The Internet, Email, GPS, Tor, Speech Recognition & Drones.
TIL - First-ever recording of a dying human brain shows waves similar to memory flashbacks
Today I learned that a species of spider discovered in 1993, a species of ant discovered in 2002, and a species of snake discovered in 2023 were all named after Harrison Ford.

TIL about the 1900 English beer poisoning. More than 6,000 people in England were poisoned by arsenic-tainted beer. It took 4 months to be noticed, with doctors initially misdiagnosing it as alcoholic neuropathy

TIL for nearly a thousand years, the ancient world’s most popular and admired comedian was Menander of Athens. Ironically, his work was lost to history until 1952, when a single play was rediscovered in Egypt intact enough to be performed
TIL that scientists found evidence that there was once a naturally occurring nuclear reactor in the region of Oklo, Gabon.
TIL that the Welwitschia mirabilis plant has a lifespan of 400 to 1500 years, with some estimated to be over 2,000 years old

TIL during the debate on the Compromise of 1850, Senator Benton charged at Senator Foote for having verbally attacked him, which caused Foote to draw a pistol at Benton. Senators intervened and Benton shouted at them to let Foote shoot him. Eventually, Senators wrested the pistol away from Foote.
Today I learned that a Jewish teenager from England was shipwrecked in South Africa, fought in Shaka Zulu’s army, and became a Zulu “king” 200 years ago.

TIL that after qualifying for the 5000m Olympic trials in 1928, black athlete Dolphus Stroud had to make his way to Boston on his own. He walked, ran, and hitch-hiked over 12 days, arriving 6 hours before his race. He collapsed due to exhaustion and malnutrition in the 6th lap
TIL in much of the U.S. "cider" normally refers to unfiltered apple juice rather than the alcoholic beverage (otherwise known as "hard cider")
TIL the share of boys and girls who say they meet up with friends almost daily outside school hours has declined by nearly 50% since the early 1990s.
TIL your gums do not grow back after receding.

TIL the word “divorce” didn’t exist in Chinese until the 19th and early 20th centuries. Prior to that, the word most often used was “dissolved”.  Men could dissolve a marriage under 7 specific conditions (ex: a lazy wife or a barren wife) while women had almost none.
TIL on the seabed between Catalina Island and the mainland lies barrels of DDT.

TIL in 1934. Mussolini ordered that the Tower of Pisa be returned to a vertical position. 361 holes were drilled into the foundations, and 90 cubic meters of concrete were poured into them. However, the result was that the tower actually sank further into the soil!
TIL Princess Anne of the British Royal family is the only member to have been convicted with a criminal offence. In 2002 she was charged and fined when one of her pet dogs attacked 2 children

TIL about Iron Mike Malloy who survived his “friends” trying to murder him for insurance money using antifreeze, turpentine, horse liniment, rat poison, wood alcohol, poisoned oysters, spoiled sardines with carpet tacks, freezing him outdoors and a hit-and-run before finally killing him with gas.
TIL Part of the reason why Thai restaurants are so common is because the Thai government paid for it.
TIL Robert Todd Lincoln, Abraham Lincoln’s eldest son, was present at 3 of the 4 presidential assassinations and was the only one of Lincoln’s 4 children to survive past 18 and outlive both parents.
TIL The Phantom is widely recognized as the first costumed superhero

TIL that Edward VII introduced “Sandringham Time” in 1901, setting all the clocks on the royal estate 30 minutes fast to provide more daylight for his favourite activity of hunting and shooting. The custom lasted 35 years until 1936, when his grandson Edward VIII literally turned back the clocks.
TIL The last unclimbed mountain in Japan was climbed in 1907 but at the top they found evidence that it had been climbed 1000 years before.
TIL IO Interactive secured the rights to make the next James Bond video game by simply presenting a Hitman game level with Daniel Craig's head replacing Agent 47's head.

TIL Germany introduced a tax on sparkling wine in 1902 to finance the nation's imperial war fleet. The tax is still being levied, with the government collecting $385 million from sparkling wine taxes in 2022
TIL the Arabic graffiti in the show Homeland were negative messages towards the show itself.
TIL that in a legal battle over the discovery of Otzi the Iceman, one claimant told the court she had spat on the 5,300-year-old mummy to “mark it” with her DNA upon her alleged discovery of it.

TIL about the Theory of multiple intelligences which posits human intelligence is not a singular general ability (g-factor) but are very distinct modalities. Musicianship, athleticism, linguistics, etc, are all forms of intelligence.
TIL the Roosevelts were known for serving "terrible" food at the White House during the Great Depression. In reality, they dined plainly as a show of solidarity with their beloved country.

TIL about the mysterious 2-mile-tall naked figure called the Marree Man that appeared in the Australian outback in 1998, carved into the earth with precise GPS mapping. Its origins remain unknown, sparking theories from artists to military involvement. The geoglyph is still visible from space
TIL tha McDonalds stopped selling salads in the USA in 2020

TIL a study found that a petri dish exposed to public bathroom air for two minutes with the hand dryers off only grew one colony of bacteria, or none at all, whereas petri dishes exposed to the hot air from a hand dryer for 30 seconds grew up to 254 colonies of bacteria.
TIL: Winston Churchill (1963) and Mother Teresa (1996) are the only two people ever granted honorary U.S. citizenship while alive.
TIL according to a 2022 survey, 90% of millennial would be willing to buy a house sight unseen

Til that Matt Hannon, star of cult movie Samurai Cop, took part in an armed robbery stealing a Rembrandt painting from televangelist Gene Scott's University Cathedral in Los Angeles. He was later arrested and sent to prison.
TIL that 80s child actor Scott Schwartz (The Toy, Kidco, A Christmas Story) later went on to appear in more than a dozen adult films
TIL some appliances have a built-in Sabbath Mode for observant Jews to use
TIL Cody Ko, a youtuber who had been canceled for having sex with a minor, is still uploading as recently as 5 hours ago (at the time of this post being made)
TIL There are more species of Sponges (7,427) than Mammals (5,046)
TIL about Acanthosis Nigricans: a disorder of brown-to-black patches of skin which indicates insulin resistance, type 2 diabetes, obesity or  hormone imbalances.
TIL the original county seat of Fresno County, CA, now sits at the bottom of an artificial reservoir.
TIL: Up until 2013, Pizza Hut was the largest buyer of kale in the U.S.
TIL Domino Pizza planned to add a dot for every new place they opened. The idea was quickly abandoned due to how fast they grew and expanded

TIL that the Pythagorean Theorem appears to have been known to the Babylonians over a thousand years before Pythagoras was born. Furthermore, the earliest surviving attributions of the theorem to Pythagoras date from about five centuries after his death.

TIL that for 30 years economists believed lending to people without collateral was mathematically impossible, but new research shows this was wrong because the original proof assumed people couldn't save money or prefer stable income, both obviously false assumptions.

TIL After the wife of Roman praetor Marcus Plautius Silvanus was found dead, having fallen from her bedroom window, Emperor Tiberius personally investigated the scene. The emperor found signs of a struggle in the room, so he compiled a report and refered Silvanus to trial for murder
TIL that during the Irish Civil War, the Irish Free State government summarily executed many Republican prisoners, including eight men who were tied to a land mine that was then detonated.
TIL That the singer of "You're a Mean One, Mr. Grinch", Thurl Ravenscroft, was also the voice of Tony the Tiger and Geoffrey the Giraffe.

TIL That in 1883, Mexican astronomer José Bonilla photographed more than 400 mysterious objects crossing the Sun over two days. Modern astrophysicists now believe he may have captured fragments of a massive comet that narrowly missed Earth

TIL that while tonka beans are prized for their flavor, it's banned in the US since 1954. The beans have the taste of vanilla, licorice, caramel, and cloves. Restaurants in the US that have the ingredient have been subject to raids and chefs relied on smugglers for the beans.

TIL José Salvador Alvarenga is the first person in recorded history to have survived in a small boat lost at sea for more than a year. He reportedly drifted across the Pacific Ocean for 438 days from 2012 to 2014, surviving on raw fish, birds, and rainwater before washing ashore in the islands
TIL that the biggest empire in the Animalia kingdom is the global mega-colony of Argentine ants spanning from California to Europe, because of matching pheronomes.
TIL Johns Hopkins Medicine got 12 men to vape salvinorin A crystals.
TIL the McDonald's "I'm Lovin' It" jingle was written by Pharrell Williams and released as a single by Justin Timberlake in 2003.

TIL that People v. Levi Weeks (1800) is considered the first murder trial in the United States to have a full, formal transcript. Weeks was accused of killing Elma Sands and was defended by Aaron Burr and Alexander Hamilton.
TIL that Jeff "Skunk" Baxter, former guitarist of Steely Dan and the Doobie Brothers, has recently served as a Congressional consultant on missile defense
TIL of a man who found $7.5 million in a "Storage Wars" unit he bought for $500. He had to negotiate with the original owners, who paid him $1.2 million to return their money.

TIL that the United States government still sends $4,500 worth of cloth to the Haudenosaunee (Iroquois) Confederacy every year, and has done so every November 11th for the past 230+ years in recognition of a still-standing treaty with them.

TIL the Paraguayan War (1864–1870) was so devastating that it wiped out 60–70% of the country's entire population, and is listed in the Guinness World Records for the highest proportional death toll from war for any single country.

TIL the experimental ‘Sprint’ anti-ballistic missile went 0 to Mach 10 in only 5 seconds, w 650,000 lbs thrust on a 7,500-lb body. For comparison, a Minuteman III ICBM makes ~200,000 lbs thrust despite weighing >10× more, and a Boeing 747-8 maxes @ ~266,000 lbs while weighing >100× more than Sprint.
TIL that 30% of adults and up to 40% of children grind or clench their teeth at night, this condition is linked not only to worn-down teeth, but also to anxiety, depression, Tmj and many more.

TIL about Coagula, DC Comics' first transgender superhero; she was introduced in a 1993 story ("The Laughing Game") where she defeats The Codpiece, a spurned-man-turned-villain with a multifunctional mechanical codpiece.
TIL in 1937 a natural gas leak caused a school to explode in Texas, killing at least 295 people. Witnesses described seeing the walls bulge and the roof lifting off the building before it collapsed.
TIL as a joke about the historic rivalry between Sweden and Denmark, the only IKEA products named in Danish are doormats and other things you walk on
TIL only 3 countries don’t use feet for flight levels (the altitude a plane flies at): China, Mongolia, and North Korea. Russia and Tajikistan use a blended system of metric at lower altitudes.

TIL Purple cabbage can be used to create a liquid that changes colors; Red, Violet, Blue, Green and Yellow depending on pH level.  This is an experiment you can easily try yourself with household materials.
TIL researchers discovered that a single 2×2 LEGO brick can handle 950 pounds of force in a hydraulic press before breaking

TIL that at an inventors' luncheon in October 1912, 24-year-old Madeleine Edison, the daughter of Thomas Edison, referred to Nikola Tesla as "our dear friend" and "the largest man I've ever seen", with Tesla standing at 6'2" tall. She described Tesla's interest in her, but she was already engaged.
TIL ‘Cummingtonite’ and was named after the town of Cummington, Massachusetts, where it was discovered in 1824.
TIL scientists have been able to trace the start of HIV/AIDS to King Leopold’s Belgian Congo, originating as far back as 1909. The first person to be infected probably got the virus in the 1920s

TIL that there is a faction of sustainability professionals named the sustainabilitists, who tackle sustainability from both the individual and the systemic standpoints (e.g., health, habits, relationships, lifestyle vs. energy, economy, environment, policy)

TIL that in 1776 a woman experienced a severe fever, and later reported their death and their body being reanimated by a genderless spirit called the Public Universal Friend. The Friend rejected pronouns, the gender binary, and preached hospitality to all.
TIL that a pinch, a dash, a smidgen, and a tad are all actual units of measure, even gets down to drops and hints.
TIL that the Washington Nationals are originally from Montreal
TIL 99% of human history happened in prehistory (before the invention of writing). It’s the longest period of human history, lasting for 3.4 million years
TIL that the Miami cannibal did not actually have bath salts in his system after toxicological testing

TIL Kate Winslet holds the Guinness World Record for longest underwater breath-hold by a lead actor (~7 minutes). She broke the record previously held by Tom Cruise.

TIL in the US, Canada and Turkey a pink battery-powered bunny is associated with Energizer - in the rest of the world the trademark belongs to Duracell. It was also trademarked by Duracell in the US, but by 1988 the trademark had expired and Energizer started using it. There have been court cases.

TIL Clowns in the UK can register with Clowns International, who will paint their appearance on a ceramic egg as a way to copyright their facial features. They have been providing this service since the 1940s and have 240 eggs in their collection.

TIL the myth that pulling out one white hair makes more grow is false. Hair follicles work independently, so plucking a white strand doesn’t spread graying or affect nearby hairs. It only grows back white because that follicle has already lost pigment.

TIL, there's an alternative to regular cremation called alkaline hydrolysis that involves being placed in a pressure vessel mixed with water and potassium hydroxide. The pressure vessel is then heated to boiling temperatures

TIL AC/DC drew only 30 people to their first UK show in April 1976. Four months of heavy touring later they broke the attendance record at London's Marquee Club - 1400 people in a 700-capacity room, beating the records of Hendrix and The Who.
